title: Acaranga Sutra: The Book of Good Conduct
description: The oldest surviving Jain text: Mahavira's teachings on non-violence, the suffering of all beings, and the ascetic path.
introduction: The Acaranga Sutra, dated to the 4th century BCE, is the oldest text in the Jain canon. It preserves the radical teachings of Mahavira on ahimsa — non-violence extended not just to humans and animals but to insects, plants, water, fire, earth, and air, all of which contain living souls. This extreme ethic of non-harm, which predates and influenced Buddhist compassion doctrine, makes the Jain tradition one of the most philosophically rigorous in the ancient world.

translation.original_text: savvesim pāṇāṇam savvesim bhūyāṇam savvesim jīvāṇam na haṃtavvā
translation.text: All breathing things, all existing things, all living beings should not be harmed.

translation.original_text: je pāṇe vahe se appāṇam vahe
translation.text: Whoever harms any living thing harms his own soul.
